content.assessment: Grade-splitting requires actors to execute a structural reorganization sequence (site selection, transport, staffing reallocation) under the same centralized authority structure that has historically driven abrupt, politically-motivated reorganizations rather than gradual piloted ones. The mechanism's reliance on concentrating specialist teachers at upper-grade sites is plausible given workforce aging and attrition data, but the proposal does not specify a phased pilot or monitoring/adaptation loop, and site-selection decisions would fall to the same centralized actor lacking a consultation architecture.
opportunities: Could be piloted regionally first in areas of high teacher shortage/aging (F-live-implementation_planning-08) before national rollout, if a phased design were added
strengths: Directly responds to the documented ageing/attrition teacher-workforce problem by concentrating specialists rather than spreading them thin (F-live-implementation_planning-08)
Splitting rather than full closure could be sequenced site-by-site, offering more pilotability than wholesale consolidation if actors choose to phase it
threats: Absent a consultation or appeals mechanism, upper-grade consolidation site choices risk provoking the same unaddressed community pushback pattern seen historically (F-live-implementation_planning-03, F-live-implementation_planning-10)

Transport frequency gaps in the most depopulating regions (North-East, South-West, West) could undermine delivery of the upper-grade commute component in exactly the areas most likely to need grade-splitting (F-live-implementation_planning-04, F-live-implementation_planning-09)
weaknesses: Site-selection and reorganization decisions remain vested in a centralized actor with no documented local consultation mechanism, raising the same procedural gap seen in 2016 flagged risks (F-live-implementation_planning-01, F-live-implementation_planning-03, F-live-implementation_planning-10)
No described monitoring or adaptation mechanism to detect and correct disruption analogous to Poland's rushed 2017 reorganization
